title: Add Haiku LLM-as-a-judge scoring for per-agent finding quality in LEARN phase
description: Add automated LLM-as-a-judge evaluation of each research agent's findings during the LEARN phase. Currently, quality metrics in run_quality are purely deterministic (counts, ratios, dedup rates). The quality_scores table in traces.db exists but is not populated with per-finding evaluations. A Haiku-based judge prompt would score each agent's batch output on 4 dimensions: factual density (specific names/numbers/dates vs vague claims), source diversity (primary vs secondary sources), novelty (genuinely new vs rehashed), and relevance (matches user.md interests). Scores stored in quality_scores feed directly into the ANALYZE phase's agent scorecards, giving the self-improvement loop a richer signal than counts alone. Haiku keeps cost under $0.02 per agent evaluation (~13 calls/day).
requirements: Create judge prompt template that scores a batch of findings on 4 dimensions (factual_density, source_diversity, novelty, relevance) each 0.0-1.0
Add judge_agent_findings() function to orchestrator/evaluator.py that calls Haiku via run_claude_prompt()
Store dimension scores in traces.db quality_scores table (one row per dimension per agent run)
Compute composite quality_score on agent_runs from dimension scores (weighted average)
Call judge_agent_findings() for each agent result during LEARN phase in runner.py
Add 'finding_judge' task_type to orchestrator/router.py with model=haiku, max_turns=5, timeout=60s
Log per-agent scores at INFO level with dimension breakdown
done_criteria: After a pipeline run, quality_scores table has 4 rows per agent (one per dimension)
agent_runs.quality_score is populated from composite judge score
ANALYZE phase agent scorecards show quality_trend from judge scores
Total judge cost per run is under $0.05 (13 agents x Haiku)
New tests verify judge prompt returns valid JSON with expected dimensions
New test verifies scores are written to quality_scores table

design_spec.architecture_impact: Adds one LLM call per agent in LEARN phase (13 Haiku calls). Populates existing quality_scores table that is currently empty. No new tables. The ANALYZE phase already reads quality_trend from agent_runs — this feature populates it with meaningful data.

context: LLM-as-a-judge is the dominant pattern for automated agent quality scoring in 2025-2026. Sources: https://www.evidentlyai.com/llm-guide/llm-as-a-judge and https://langfuse.com/guides/cookbook/example_pydantic_ai_mcp_agent_evaluation (multi-level agent evaluation). MindPattern's ANALYZE phase already consumes quality_trend from agent_runs but the data is sparse. This feature fills the gap with cheap, structured evaluation that makes the self-improvement loop more effective.

feedback_history.reason: Codex adversarial: 4 files, M effort, requires creating LLM judge prompts + new Haiku calls + storing scores; too ambitious for fix agent
